Formula: Sleeper's `settings.waiver_bid` for the winning waiver claim. 0 for free-agent / commissioner adds.
Notes: Only populated for type=waiver rows. Free-agent adds don't go through FAAB.

Formula: Sum of `waiver_bid` across every claim (winning + losing) for this player in this week's waiver run.
Notes: Captures total FAAB the league burned on the player. The winning bid is the top of this stack.

Formula: winning_bid − max(other valid bids ≤ winning_bid).
Notes: Bids strictly greater than the winning bid are excluded — they were invalidated (roster full, insufficient FAAB) and don't represent real competition. Blank for uncontested waivers.

Formula: (winning_bid − runner_up) / winning_bid × 100.
Notes: How much of the winning bid was a premium over the runner-up, normalized by bid size so it's comparable across big and small auctions (a $50-over-$40 win = the same 20% as $5-over-$4). Bounded 0–100; 100 vs a $0 runner-up. Blank only when there was no valid runner-up. Replaces the old 'FAAB % difference over second place' (which divided by the runner-up and blew up / was undefined for small or $0 runner-ups).

Formula: Count of every waiver attempt (complete + failed) on this player in this week's waiver run.
Notes: Includes losing bids — describes interest, not auction wins.

Formula: Forward-looking. Mean fantasy points for the added player over the NFL games that fall between this pickup's Date and Date dropped/traded (open-ended if the player is still rostered). Uses nflverse fantasy_points_ppr; falls back to pw when nflverse has no record.
Notes: Captures what the added player actually delivered while on THIS team. Includes all NFL weeks in the tenure window, whether the player was started or benched.

Stat: Average PPG of dropped player over same time
Formula: Mean fantasy points for the dropped player over the SAME tenure window as Average PPG on team (pickup → next drop/trade of the added player). Sourced from nflverse so it covers the dropped player even after they left this team's roster.
Notes: Lets you compare 'what we got' vs 'what we'd have gotten by keeping him'. Blank when no dropped player or no NFL games in the window.

Formula: Mean fantasy points across the added player's 5 most-recent PLAYED NFL games BEFORE the pickup date. If fewer than 5 played games exist on record, averages whatever's available (2 games → mean of 2).
Notes: Trailing snapshot of the player's NFL form at the time of pickup. Used for cuff detection and as a backward-looking complement to 'Average PPG on team'. Sourced from nflverse so unrostered weeks count.

Formula: Average PPG on team − Average PPG of dropped player over same time. Both numbers use the forward-looking tenure window.
Notes: Positive = the pickup outperformed the dropped player over the time he was rostered. Treats a missing side as 0; this can over- or under-state when only one side resolves.

Stat: Difference of averages adjusted by position
Formula: added_adj − dropped_adj, where adj = (forward-looking tenure PPG) × all_starter_avg / pos_avg[player_position]. all_starter_avg and pos_avg are league-wide all-time starter averages from player_week.
Notes: Normalises QB/RB/WR/TE scoring scales so a 12-PPG TE doesn't read the same as a 12-PPG QB. Uses forward-looking PPG (what the player did on this team), not pre-pickup snapshot.

Formula: added_player_age − dropped_player_age, in years (decimal), computed at the pickup date from each player's birth_date.
Notes: Negative when the team replaced an older player with a younger one (typical 'getting younger' move).

Formula: True if the picking team rostered another player who (a) is STILL on the roster at the pickup week, (b) was a STARTER in any of the previous 3 weeks (the pickup week and the two before it), and (c) plays the same NFL team, (d) same NFL position, and (e) averaged at least 10 PPG more than the added player over the last 5 played games.
Notes: Handcuff detection. Uses a 3-week starter window (so a cuff added right after the starter goes down still registers) BUT the reference player must still be rostered at pickup — a teammate dropped before the pickup is no longer insurance you hold (Item 8).

Formula: Count of player_week rows for (Team, Player Added) with Starter/Bench != 'Starter' between the pickup date and the player's first start on this team.
Notes: Bounded above by Date dropped/traded — if the player was let go before ever starting, this column is blank.

Stat: Number of starts before next drop
Formula: Count of player_week rows for (Team, Player Added) with Starter/Bench == 'Starter' between Date and Date dropped/traded.
Notes: If the player was never dropped, counts all starts through end of dataset.

Formula: Days the ADDED player spent on this team: from the pickup Date to the next drop/trade off this team (Date dropped/traded), or to today if still rostered. Blank when the transaction has no added player (a pure drop) — no player whose tenure to measure; a genuine 0-day tenure (added then immediately moved) stays 0.
Notes: Calendar-day span of the tenure window used by the forward-looking PPG / Points Added metrics.

Formula: (Number of starts before next drop) / (weeks rostered between Date and Date dropped/traded).
Notes: Includes bye and injury weeks in the denominator — measures gross start rate.

Stat: Injury adjusted % of starts made while rostered
Formula: Like '% of starts made while rostered' but exclude weeks where Bye? or Injury? is True from BOTH numerator and denominator.
Notes: Measures start rate when the player was actually available.

Formula: adjusted_diff × (1 + pct_starts) × (1 + pct_starts_injury_adjusted) + CUFF_BONUS. adjusted_diff is 'Difference of averages adjusted by position'. pct_starts is '% of starts made while rostered'. pct_starts_injury_adjusted is the injury-adjusted variant. CUFF_BONUS = 5 PPG when 'Cuff at time of pickup?' = True, else 0. An added player who was NEVER rostered for a full NFL week = 0 (added nothing measurable). Blank only for a pure drop (no added player).
Notes: Composite metric blending pure PPG difference with playing-time leverage and handcuff insurance. Tune CUFF_BONUS by editing the constant in src/lotg.py.

Formula: The ADDED player's fantasy points summed over the weeks they STARTED for this team, from pickup until their next exit (drop/trade). 0 if no add.
Notes: Started weeks come from player_week (Starter/Bench == Starter), bounded by the pickup→next-exit window.

Formula: The DROPPED player's real NFL fantasy points (nflverse game log; 0 for any bye/injury/DNP week) summed over exactly the weeks the ADDED player started — the opportunity cost of starting the add over the drop. 0 if no drop or no add.
Notes: The dropped player's counterfactual points come from the game log regardless of where they actually landed.

Formula: Points Added − Points Lost (0 for a pure drop) — the net started-week production the swap gained the team.
Notes: Positive = the add out-scored the player it replaced over the shared started weeks.

Formula: Points Added / number of started weeks in the window (0 when none) — comparable across swaps of different lengths.
Notes: Per-start average of the added side.

Formula: Points Lost / number of started weeks in the window (0 when none).
Notes: Per-start average of the lost (dropped-player) side.

Formula: Net points / number of started weeks in the window (0 when none).
Notes: Per-start net of the swap.

Stat: Avg points added adjusted by position
Formula: Avg points added with each week scaled by the ADDED player's position (× league_starter_avg / pos_avg) before averaging, so cross-position swaps compare.
Notes: Position-normalised variant of Avg points added.

Stat: Avg points lost adjusted by position
Formula: Avg points lost with each week scaled by the DROPPED player's position (× league_starter_avg / pos_avg) before averaging.
Notes: Position-normalised variant of Avg points lost.

Stat: Avg net points adjusted by position
Formula: Avg points added adjusted by position − Avg points lost adjusted by position — the position-normalised net of the swap.
Notes: Position-normalised variant of Avg net points.

Stat: KTC value of player added at deal time
Formula: The ADDED player's KTC superflex value (sf_trade_value) on the pickup date.
Notes: Sourced from dynasty-daddy's KTC history (matches keeptradecut.com); superflex because the league is 2QB. History back to ≈ April 2021; blank before that or for an untracked player.

Stat: KTC value of player dropped at deal time
Formula: The DROPPED player's KTC superflex value (sf_trade_value) on the transaction date.
Notes: Blank for a pure add, an untracked player, or a pre-April-2021 date.

Stat: Net KTC value at deal time
Formula: (KTC of added player) − (KTC of dropped player) on the deal date; a missing side counts as 0. Positive = the team gained KTC value on the swap.
Notes: Superflex values (sf_trade_value).

Stat: KTC value of player added at end of season
Formula: The ADDED player's KTC superflex value at this season's end (the Monday after the fantasy championship — the day after NFL week-17 Sunday).
Notes: Blank for a future season end or pre-history date.

Stat: KTC value of player added 1 year later
Formula: The ADDED player's KTC superflex value exactly one calendar year after the transaction date.
Notes: Blank when that date is still in the future.

Stat: KTC value of player added 2 years later
Formula: The ADDED player's KTC superflex value exactly two calendar years after the transaction date.
Notes: Blank when that date is still in the future.

Stat: KTC value of player dropped at end of season
Formula: The DROPPED player's KTC superflex value at this season's end (Monday after the fantasy championship).
Notes: Shows whether the drop turned out to be a mistake. Blank for a future/pre-history date or a pure add.

Stat: KTC value of player dropped 1 year later
Formula: The DROPPED player's KTC superflex value exactly one year after the transaction date.
Notes: Blank when still in the future or a pure add.

Stat: KTC value of player dropped 2 years later
Formula: The DROPPED player's KTC superflex value exactly two years after the transaction date.
Notes: Blank when still in the future or a pure add.

Stat: Net KTC value at end of season
Formula: (added player KTC) − (dropped player KTC) at this season's end; missing side = 0. Positive = the team came out ahead in KTC by season's end.
Notes: Superflex values.

Formula: (added player KTC) − (dropped player KTC) one year after the deal; missing side = 0.
Notes: Superflex values; blank inputs treated as 0.

Formula: (added player KTC) − (dropped player KTC) two years after the deal; missing side = 0.
Notes: Superflex values; blank inputs treated as 0.

Formula: The player this move ADDED to the team (the waiver claim / free-agent / commissioner add). Blank on a pure-drop row.
Notes: Hyperlink target to the player's player_all_time row (Phase 11D).

Formula: The player this move DROPPED from the team (cut to make room, or the FA-drop side). Blank when nothing was dropped.
Notes: Hyperlink target to the player's player_all_time row (Phase 11D).

Formula: How the add happened: a WAIVER claim (FAAB bid), a FREE-AGENT pickup (no bid), or a COMMISSIONER add.
Notes: Drives whether the FAAB columns are populated (waivers only).

Formula: The first date this team later dropped or traded the ADDED player, from the team×player movement log.
Notes: Blank if the player is still rostered or the row wasn't an add.

Formula: Row pointer to the ADDED player's NEXT event anywhere in the league (across teams, including trades): '#N' = transactions.csv row, 'T#N' = trades.csv row, 'PH#N' = picks.csv row (1-indexed). A clickable hyperlink in the xlsx.
Notes: Multi-row trades count as ONE event (the link skips the deal's other-team rows). Blank at the end of the player's chain or when there's no added player.

Formula: Row pointer to the ADDED player's PREVIOUS event anywhere in the league (same '#N' / 'T#N' / 'PH#N' scheme). A drafted player's chain STARTS at their picks draft row, so the first-ever event points back to 'PH#N'.
Notes: Blank at the start of the chain or when there's no added player.

Formula: Row pointer to the DROPPED player's NEXT event anywhere in the league (same scheme). A clickable hyperlink in the xlsx.
Notes: Blank at the end of the chain or when there's no dropped player.

Formula: Row pointer to the DROPPED player's PREVIOUS event anywhere in the league (same scheme).
Notes: Blank at the start of the chain or when there's no dropped player.

Stat: Number of times picked up by this team
Formula: Running 1-indexed counter of acquisitions of (Team, Player) in chronological order — INCLUDING trades (a player received in a trade counts as a pickup), interleaved with waiver/FA adds by date.
Notes: If a team picks up the same player twice (by any mix of add or trade-in), the second reads 2.

Stat: Number of times dropped by this team
Formula: Running 1-indexed counter of departures of (Team, Player Dropped) in chronological order — INCLUDING trades away (a player traded out counts as a drop).
Notes: Mirror of 'Number of times picked up by this team'. Blank on a transaction row that didn't drop anyone (a pure pickup).

Formula: Marginal CHANGE in the team's Tanking score caused by THIS move, holding everything else constant: (1/6)*Δt3_age + (1/9)*Δfuture_cap. PF/MaxPF terms are unaffected by a roster move so they drop out. Δt3_age = -(avg_age_post - avg_age_pre)/(L_AvgAge - 21), where avg_age is 'Team age including picks' (entities = rostered players + future picks); avg_age_pre = A (the team's roster age that week, from team_week), N = that week's entity count, and avg_age_post = (N*A - Σsent_age + Σrecv_age)/(N - k_sent + k_recv). For transactions the assets are the added/dropped players; for trades they are the players AND picks received/sent (picks use expected future-rookie age). Δfuture_cap = round-weighted future picks received - sent ({R1:0.25, R2:0.09, R3:0.03, R4:0.01}, only picks 1-3 seasons out); waiver/FA transactions move no picks so this is 0. Week-of-move derived from Date (Sept 7 = wk1, capped 1..17), with adjacent-week fallback for A/N.
Notes: Pre/post-transaction tanking delta (replaces the old per-week team-level lookup). Positive = the move made the team younger and/or richer in picks (more tanking); negative = dealt picks/youth for win-now talent. Current-year rookie picks aren't tradeable mid-season, so the t4 (this-year pick) term is ~0 and omitted.

Formula: Semicolon-joined list of everything that came TO this team in the trade: players, draft picks ('2025 1.05(B. Robinson)'), and FAAB ('$N FAAB'). FAAB is rendered PER SENDER (one '$N FAAB' per source roster), so a multi-sender 3-team deal shows e.g. '$4 FAAB' + '$15 FAAB' rather than a lumped '$19 FAAB'.
Notes: FAAB capture (Phase 7A) makes FAAB-only trades show real assets. A future pick is shown by its original owner ('2027 2(Oliverwkw)'), a made pick by the player it became.

Formula: Semicolon-joined list of everything this team GAVE UP in the trade — attributed to the roster that actually gave up each asset (the player it dropped, the pick's previous owner, the FAAB sender), so a 3+ team trade lists each asset exactly once per side instead of every team claiming the whole pot. FAAB is summed per sending roster.
Notes: Net-zero swaps (nothing actually changed hands — no players/picks, FAAB nets to 0 for every roster) are deleted entirely from trades.csv and all trade counts. The two sides mirror and dollars conserve.

Formula: Count of the assets this team RECEIVED in the trade — every entry in 'Assets received' that is a player or a draft pick. FAAB ('$N FAAB') is money, not an asset, so it is excluded.
Notes: Non-FAAB count of the received side. A FAAB-only haul reads 0.

Formula: Count of the assets this team GAVE UP in the trade — every entry in 'Assets sent' that is a player or a draft pick. FAAB ('$N FAAB') is money, not an asset, so it is excluded.
Notes: Non-FAAB count of the sent side. A FAAB-only give-up reads 0.

Stat: Total number of assets in trade
Formula: Total players and draft picks that changed hands in the WHOLE trade, counting every asset once no matter which team sent or received it. FAAB is excluded. For a 3-team deal this is the entire pot, so every row of the same trade shows the same number.
Notes: Whole-trade total (all teams), identical across the rows of one multi-team trade — not just the current row's received + sent.

Formula: An overall measure of how impactful/good a trade was for the team — a composite, with WIN IMPACT heavily weighted, of five standardised (z-scored across all trades) player/asset signals (no KTC in the output), summed and scaled (×0.6) into a WIN-IMPACT band — small numbers that read as an added win total (≈ −4 .. +16 across the league, most trades within ±2), not a KTC-like magnitude. (1) WIN IMPACT, weight 2.0 — games the trade flipped PLUS a share of games flipped by later trades that re-used the received assets. Direct: for each post-trade week (over the player's whole tenure, NOT a 10-game window) a received asset started, net points = received starters − the top-k players traded away; if (actual weekly margin) vs (margin − net) crosses 0, ±1 game. Downstream: when a received asset is later re-traded, this trade is credited that later trade's win impact × the FRACTION of the later trade's sent-side KTC value (on the later trade's day) that came from this trade's assets — recursively, so a minor add-on bundled later for a stud earns only its small value share. (2) realized production, Avg net points, 0.8; (3) trade value incl. picks, Trade addition value, 0.5; (4) future capital, Pick value received, 0.5; (5) youth, −Asset difference in average age, 0.3. Continuous → percentile-ranks cleanly.
Notes: Item 3. Renamed from 'Team performance improvement' (it blends win impact with trade value, so it's a trade-impact/quality index, not a pure on-field-improvement metric — and pure performance improvement is inherently at odds with not punishing tank trades). Win impact uses nflverse points + team_week margins; KTC only proportions the downstream credit, not the output. Scaled ×0.6 into an added-win-total band (was ×1500, KTC-like); the rescale is a pure linear/monotonic transform, so ranking and the O-Score percentile that consumes this are unchanged. Weights/scale tunable; games-flipped is the heavily-weighted core. ≈0.26 correlation with 'KTC value difference at end of season'.

Formula: An overall 0–100 score for the move: take FOUR stats, convert each to its PERCENTILE (0–100) across that sheet's rows, and average the four. The four (per sheet): picks → Avg points added, Pick-adjusted Difference in Player addition value, most-recent PICK-ADJUSTED-KTC difference (so market value is judged vs the draft-slot window, not absolute; points added stays absolute), Pick-adjusted Difference in Avg career PPG adjusted by position; transactions → Avg net points, Player addition value, most-recent-KTC, % of starts made while rostered; trades → Avg net points, Trade addition value, most-recent-KTC, Trade impact score. 'most-recent-KTC' = the percentile of the latest populated KTC value/difference on the row (picks scan 4yr→3yr→2yr→1yr→end-of-rookie→draft day; transactions/trades scan their 2yr→1yr→end-of-season→deal day). Percentiles use average tie-handling EXCEPT a value of exactly 0 (no production — e.g. the ~87% of adds that never started) is pushed to the bottom of its tie, so zeros cluster low rather than in the middle. O-Score = the average of the AVAILABLE percentiles: a 'droppable' component may be missing (on picks the pick-adjusted Player addition value of a player never rostered for a full week is dropped and the other three averaged), but every REQUIRED component must be present.
Notes: Item 4. ~50 = middle-of-the-pack, ~80+ a strong move. PURE DROPS (drop-only transactions) get their own 0–50 score: the average percentile rank — computed only against the other pure drops — of (1) most-recent populated Net KTC (0 when none), (2) Dropped avg points, (3) Dropped total points, (4) Player addition value, DIVIDED BY TWO, so a perfect drop tops out at 50 and never out-scores a good add; the zero-to-bottom tie rule doesn't apply there (0 post-drop points = the best outcome). Other rows' scores are unaffected; pure drops ARE folded into the Transaction-skill aggregate, but each counts only 1/3 as much as an add/swap. Blank only for: vet / unmade picks; trades whose KTC is blank (one-sided, only untracked assets received); and retired / untracked players (no KTC). For picks, the two non-rookie drafts (the 2020 ESPN startup draft and the 2021 supplemental veteran draft) are scored only in their OWN percentile pool, kept out of the rookie-pick pool; in practice every one of those rows ends up blank. Percentiles are within each sheet (picks vs picks, etc.).

Formula: The DROPPED player's average NFL fantasy points over his next 17 PLAYED games from the drop date on (nflverse log, league scoring), NEGATED — points-per-game that walked out the door. Dynamic: with fewer than 17 post-drop games played so far it averages what exists; a real 0 means the player never played again (the perfect drop).
Notes: Populated for every row with a dropped player (swaps too); blank only when nothing was dropped. Feeds the pure-drop O-Score. More negative = worse drop.

Formula: The DROPPED player's TOTAL NFL fantasy points over the same next-17-played-games window, NEGATED. Dynamic like Dropped avg points; a real 0 means no post-drop games played.
Notes: Total companion to Dropped avg points; feeds the pure-drop O-Score. More negative = more points given away.

Stat: KTC value difference at deal time
Formula: (depth-adjusted KTC of received assets) − (depth-adjusted KTC of sent assets) on the deal date, superflex values. DEPTH TAX: on each side the assets' KTC are sorted descending, the BEST counts in full and each next is discounted geometrically (2nd ×0.6, 3rd ×0.6², …) so stacking pieces isn't over-credited (you can only start so many). A 1-for-1 equals the raw difference; a 3-scrubs-for-1-stud package is taxed. FAAB counts at the league-avg KTC-per-$.
Notes: Replaces the naive Σreceived − Σsent; depth factor 0.6 is tunable. Covers players, picks (dynasty-daddy round labels), and FAAB. Positive = this team won the deal's value on day one.

Stat: KTC value difference at end of season
Formula: The same depth-adjusted received − sent KTC difference, re-evaluated at this season's end (the Monday after the fantasy championship). Shows how the deal looked once the season played out.
Notes: Superflex KTC, depth-taxed; blank inputs treated per the deal-time rule. Future/pre-history dates are blank.

Formula: The depth-adjusted received − sent KTC difference exactly one year after the trade — who came out ahead by realized dynasty value a year on.
Notes: Superflex KTC, depth-taxed. Blank when that date is still in the future.

Formula: The depth-adjusted received − sent KTC difference exactly two years after the trade — the long-run verdict on who won.
Notes: Superflex KTC, depth-taxed. Blank when that date is still in the future.

Formula: Σ(KTC value of just the pick assets received) at deal time. Players excluded.
Notes: Highlights pick-heavy trades. Blank when the trade had no picks on the received side.

Stat: Change in pick value at draft time
Formula: For each '??'-slot pick received: (KTC at Sept 1 of pick's draft year — the post-draft snapshot once the pick resolved into a rookie) − (KTC at deal time). Sum across picks.
Notes: Captures whether the team did better or worse than the at-trade generic estimate once the slot was actually drawn. Picks whose drafts are still in the future don't contribute.

Formula: Of the assets this team received in this trade, those they currently hold (no subsequent trade-out AND no subsequent free-agent drop). Pick labels display as 'YYYY R.SLOT(F. Last)' for drafted picks, generic for not-yet-drafted.
Notes: V2 of the chain — drops to FA are now broken out into their own column.

Formula: Of the assets this team received in this trade, those whose first subsequent exit event was a TRADE (not a drop to FA).
Notes: Determined by whichever exit event came first: trade vs FA drop. Earliest event wins.

Formula: Of the assets this team received in this trade, those whose first subsequent exit event was a DROP to free agency (via transactions.csv, not a trade). Picks excluded (picks can't be dropped to FA).
Notes: Joined off the transactions drop log: any 'Player Dropped' entry from this team after the trade date. New in V2 of the chain.

Formula: Aggregated received-side of the NEXT trade(s) where this team gave up any asset from this trade's received side.
Notes: 'What this trade's haul turned into one hop later'. Pick labels resolve to specific slot + drafted player where available, e.g. '2024 1.05(B. Robinson)'.

Stat: Additional assets traded away in those deals
Formula: Other assets given up in those immediate-next trades alongside this trade's haul.
Notes: Quantifies the cost of converting the haul. Dedups across multiple downstream hops.

Stat: Return from trades of trades...of trades. Keep going until present day
Formula: Recursive walk: follow each received asset through every subsequent trade by this team, collecting received assets at each hop. Terminates when an asset has no further trade-out event.
Notes: Full chain. Picks display with their drafted player when known.

Formula: mean(age of received assets at trade date) − mean(age of sent assets at trade date). Players use Sleeper's birth_date. Picks count as future rookies: synthetic birth_date = Sept 1 of (pick_year − 22), so a 2026 pick traded mid-2025 'expects' a ~21-year-old rookie. Earlier trades of further-out picks come out younger, which lines up with intuition.
Notes: Negative = team got younger. NFL rookies average ~22 at draft time; the Sept 1 anchor matches our late-August league rookie draft. Never blank (Phase 7C): when one side has no aged asset (FAAB-only or an empty give-away side) there is no measurable age differential, so it reports 0.

Formula: Count of distinct teams in the trade = this team + every counterparty. 2 for a normal swap, 3+ for a multi-team trade.
Notes: Phase 7B. Derived from Sleeper's roster_ids on the trade transaction.

Stat: Link to next transaction per asset
Formula: For each asset RECEIVED (same order as 'Assets received'), a row pointer to that asset's NEXT event — '#N' = transactions.csv, 'T#N' = trades.csv, 'PH#N' = picks.csv — as a ';'-joined list aligned 1:1 with the received assets. Players follow the shared player chain; picks follow a separate pick chain to the next trade that moved them (and TERMINATE at the pick's draft row); FAAB carries blank.
Notes: In the xlsx this is exploded into one clickable column per received asset (cell shows the asset name, links to its next event); the CSV keeps the ';'-joined ref list. The pick chain never crosses into the drafted player.

Stat: Link to previous transaction per asset
Formula: For each asset RECEIVED, a row pointer to that asset's PREVIOUS event (same '#N' / 'T#N' / 'PH#N' scheme), ';'-joined and aligned 1:1 with 'Assets received'. Built from the received side only, so the two mirror rows of one trade event don't link to each other.
Notes: Exploded into one clickable column per received asset in the xlsx. A pick's chain starts from the trade that first moved it; a drafted player's chain starts at the draft row that the pick chain terminates on.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Per (team, year, week): mean over rostered player ages AND future picks held by this team at that week's date. Pick ages use _pick_expected_age — synthetic birth date Sept 1 of (pick_year − 22). Per-week pick ownership is tracked by walking pick_trade_events with their real trade dates. Commissioner-moved picks (in traded_picks but no matching trade event) are treated as 'always with the current owner'. team_year / team_all_time / league_* aggregations average team_week values.
Notes: Used in the tanking calculation in place of the rostered-only age average. A team accumulating future draft capital reads younger — which is the tank signal we want.

Stat: Avg PPG of received players on team
Formula: Per received player, their mean fantasy_points_ppr over NFL games from trade date through next drop/trade from this team. ALSO includes received DRAFT PICKS (Phase 7D): the player drafted with the pick contributes their mean PPG over their post-draft tenure on this team (draft ≈ late August of the pick year → next exit) — but only when this team actually made the selection (picks Team == this team); a pick flipped before the draft, or a not-yet-drafted future pick, contributes nothing. Aggregated as the mean across all these received assets.
Notes: Forward-looking — actual production while on this team, players and drafted picks alike. Sourced from nflverse, so injured/bye/suspended weeks (no game log row) are already excluded; only games actually played count.

Stat: Avg PPG of sent players over same time
Formula: Per sent player, mean fantasy_points_ppr over the COLLECTIVE tenure span of the received players (trade date through latest drop of any received player). Aggregated as mean across sent players.
Notes: Measures 'what we'd have gotten by keeping the sent players over the same span'.

Stat: Avg PPG of received players in 5 games before trade
Formula: Per received player, mean fantasy_points_ppr over their 5 most-recent NFL games before trade date. Aggregated as mean across received players. <5 games → average what's available.
Notes: Backward-looking snapshot of received players' form at trade time.

Formula: Avg PPG of received players on team − Avg PPG of sent players over same time — how much better (or worse) the received side produced per game than the side given up, over the forward-looking window.
Notes: Positive = the received players out-scored the sent players per game.

Stat: Difference of averages adjusted by position
Formula: The same received − sent PPG gap, but each side's PPG is first position-normalised (× league_starter_avg / pos_avg) so a cross-position trade is judged on positional value rather than raw points.
Notes: Position-normalised variant of the trades Difference of averages.

Formula: V2 composite mirroring the transaction 'Player addition value': adj_diff × (1 + pct_starts) × (1 + pct_starts_injury_adjusted) + CUFF_BONUS(5). adj_diff = 'Difference of averages adjusted by position' (received-side adjusted on-team PPG − sent-side adjusted PPG; a side with no players contributes 0). pct_starts = average over the RECEIVED players of their '% of starts made while rostered' on this team over their post-trade tenure (trade → next exit); pct_starts_injury_adjusted divides starts by injury/bye-free weeks only. CUFF_BONUS (+5) is added once if ANY received player was a cuff at the trade — the team already rostered a STARTER on the same NFL team + position (still rostered at the trade week) whose last-5 PPG was 10+ above the received player's last-5, the same handcuff test as the transaction 'Cuff at time of pickup?'. PLUS a pick-value term: future picks (next 3 seasons only) on each side are valued with the SAME round weights tanking uses (1st=0.25, 2nd=0.09, 3rd=0.03, 4th=0.01) and the received−sent difference is scaled by a coefficient (currently 20 → a future 1st ≈ +5, about one cuff bonus) and added in, so pick-heavy hauls register. The pick term applies even when adj_diff is None, so a pick-only haul is no longer flat 0.
Notes: Item 7E. Players DRAFTED with received picks feed adj_diff (Phase 7D); the pick-value term only counts FUTURE (next-3-season) capital, so a current-season pick that gets drafted isn't double-counted. Playing-time leverage is over received players only. The pick coefficient (20) is tunable.

Formula: Received assets = received players + the players THIS team drafted with received picks (window starts at the draft). For each NFL week let k = how many received assets STARTED for this team; Points added += the sum of those k starters' points.
Notes: Started weeks/points come from player_week.

Formula: For each week (with k received starters), the sum of the TOP-k players-traded-away by their real NFL points that week (each sent asset counted at most once/week, capped at the number sent) — the best plays you forwent. Sent picks contribute the player drafted with them.
Notes: Top-k 'maximize' rule generalizes the 1-for-1 transaction Points Lost to multi-asset trades; counterfactual points from the nflverse game log.

Formula: Points added − Points lost — the net started-week production this team gained from the deal.
Notes: Positive = the received side out-produced the best-k sent assets over the matched weeks.

Formula: Points added / number of matched weeks (weeks with ≥1 received starter).
Notes: Per-matched-week average of the received side.

Formula: Points lost / number of matched weeks.
Notes: Per-matched-week average of the forgone (sent) side.

Formula: Net points / number of matched weeks.
Notes: Per-matched-week net of the deal.

Stat: Avg points added adjusted by position
Formula: Avg points added with each received asset's points scaled by its own position (× league_starter_avg / pos_avg) before summing — comparable across positions.
Notes: Position-normalised variant of Avg points added.

Stat: Avg points lost adjusted by position
Formula: Avg points lost with each sent asset's points position-scaled before summing. The lost side keeps the same top-k assets chosen by raw points, then sums their position-adjusted points.
Notes: Position-normalised variant of Avg points lost.

Stat: Avg net points adjusted by position
Formula: Avg points added adjusted by position − Avg points lost adjusted by position — the position-normalised net of the deal.
Notes: Position-normalised variant of Avg net points.

Formula: Canonical pick notation 'round.position' (e.g. '1.05' = round 1, slot 5): position is the pick's place in DRAFT ORDER within the round (2.01 = first pick of round 2). LINEAR drafts: position == draft_slot. SNAKE drafts (the 2021 rookie drafts) reverse even rounds, so draft_slot 1 picks last in round 2 (2.08) and first in round 3 (3.01); position = team_count + 1 − draft_slot on even rounds. A FUTURE pick's draft order isn't finalized, so on its own row the slot shows as '??' ('2.??') — the Original Team column identifies it — and resolves to the real slot once that season drafts.
Notes: Earlier builds labelled every round by raw draft_slot, which mis-numbered even rounds of the 2021 snake drafts (e.g. Trey Sermon showed 2.05 instead of 2.04). Player / Original Team / chain stay keyed by draft_slot — only the displayed number follows draft order. In references elsewhere (trades.csv, history comments) a MADE pick is substituted with the player it became ('2024 1.05(B. Robinson)') and a FUTURE pick is shown by its original owner ('2027 2(Oliverwkw)'). SYNTHETIC draft-day picks: '2.09' is the toilet-bracket reward pick (2024+), drafted player = the FIRST commissioner-forced add on draft day, original team = the prior season's losers-bracket (toilet) champion, final team = whoever it was force-added to. '5.01, 5.02, …' are the 20-FAAB draft-day buys (2025+), original = final = buyer. For slot-based comparisons (pick-adjusted differences, Draft Value), 2.09 counts as a 2.08 and each 5.0X as a 4.08.

Formula: The roster that ORIGINALLY owned this pick before any trades — i.e. the team in that draft-position slot per Sleeper's slot_to_roster_id mapping. For traded picks, this is the chain origin (the team whose own pick this is).
Notes: Distinct from 'Team'. ESPN-era picks (moved before Sleeper's tracking window) fall back to the slot owner; if that's also unavailable, the picker is used.

Sheet: player_week / team_week / team_year / team_all_time / transactions / trades / picks
Formula: The roster that actually MADE the selection (= last owner in the trade chain). Equals Original Team when the pick wasn't traded. (Formerly 'Final Team'.)
Notes: Pulled from Sleeper draft picks (roster_id field) and from the end of the reconstructed trade chain. For 2021 rookie-draft EVEN rounds (whose Sleeper picker data is corrupted), this is repaired from the roster ledger — the team that actually first rostered the drafted player.

Formula: Days the DRAFTED player stayed on the team that drafted it (Team): from the draft anchor (≈ Aug 28 of the pick year, after offseason pick trades, before the rookie draft) to that player's next exit (drop/trade) off the team, or to today if still rostered. Blank for an unmade pick (a future pick with no player selected yet — 'Unknown') — no player whose tenure to measure, mirroring a transactions pure drop; every MADE pick is a number ≥ 0 (a genuine 0-day tenure, or a pick whose player can't be mapped to a game log, falls back to 0).
Notes: Pick analogue of the transactions 'Length of tenure on team'. Uses the same next-exit lookup; the draft anchor matches the 7D drafted-pick PPG window.

Formula: Mean fantasy_points_ppr of the DRAFTED player over the NFL games they played while on the team that drafted them (Team), in the tenure window: draft anchor (≈ Aug 28 of the pick year) → next exit off the team (or today). Blank ONLY when the player was never on the team's roster for an NFL week (cut after the draft before week 1) — or for an unmade pick. If the player was rostered for ≥1 NFL week but logged no games (injured/inactive all tenure), it's 0, not blank.
Notes: Pick analogue of the transactions 'Average PPG on team' / the 7D drafted-pick PPG; built on the nflverse game log (games actually played, so injured/bye/suspended weeks are excluded). Roster presence comes from player_week (starter or bench).

Stat: Avg PPG on team adjusted by position
Formula: Avg PPG on team × league_starter_avg / pos_avg[player_position] — normalises QB/RB/WR/TE scoring scales (a 12-PPG TE ≠ a 12-PPG QB). Blank / 0 exactly when Avg PPG on team is.
Notes: Same position normaliser used across transactions/trades adjusted metrics.

Formula: Mean fantasy_points_ppr of the DRAFTED player over every NFL game they played FROM THE DRAFT ONWARD (across all teams, not scoped to the drafting team). Vets are treated as rookies: only post-draft games count, so a startup/vet-draft player's pre-draft history is excluded. Injury-adjusted by construction (the nflverse log only has games played). NEVER blank for a made pick — a player with no post-draft games (e.g. a vet drafted at the end of his career who never played again) is 0. Blank only for an unmade pick (no player).
Notes: Per user: the player's rate since being drafted (how the pick panned out), distinct from 'Avg PPG on team' (only their tenure on the drafting team). 'Career' is bounded below by the draft and by nflverse coverage (2021+).

Stat: Avg career PPG adjusted by position
Formula: Avg career PPG × league_starter_avg / pos_avg[player_position] — same position normaliser as the other adjusted metrics. 0 / blank exactly when Avg career PPG is.
Notes: Position-normalised post-draft rate.

Formula: The drafted player's age in years (decimal) at the draft anchor (≈ Aug 28 of the pick year), from their birth_date. Blank for an unmade pick or a player with no birth_date on record.
Notes: How old the player a pick became was on draft day.

Formula: Mirror of the transactions composite, with an ON-TEAM baseline (a pick gives up no player, so there is no 'dropped' side): (Avg PPG on team adjusted by position) × (1 + % of starts made while rostered by drafting team) × (1 + injury-adjusted % of starts) + CUFF_BONUS(5 when Cuff when drafted? is True). Blank when there is no on-team production to value (the player was never on the drafting team's roster for an NFL week) or for an unmade pick.
Notes: Per user: on-team baseline. Same CUFF_BONUS and leverage shape as the transactions 'Player addition value'.

Formula: True if, at the drafted player's FIRST week on the drafting team's roster, the team already rostered a STARTER on the same NFL team + position whose last-5 PPG was 10+ above them — the same handcuff test as 'Cuff at time of pickup?'. Evaluated at the first rostered week (not the draft date) because a rookie's NFL team/position isn't known until they actually play. False if the player was never rostered here (or an unmade pick).
Notes: Feeds the CUFF_BONUS in Player addition value.

Formula: Number of weeks the drafted player was on the drafting team's roster (starter or bench) before their FIRST start for that team, within the post-draft tenure window. Blank if the player never started for the drafting team (or an unmade pick).
Notes: Pick analogue of the transactions 'Weeks between pickup and start'.

Stat: Number of starts before next transaction
Formula: Count of weeks the drafted player STARTED for the drafting team between the draft and their next exit off that team (trade/drop). A number ≥ 0 for every made pick (0 if they never started here); blank only for an unmade pick.
Notes: Pick analogue of the transactions 'Number of starts before next drop'.

Stat: % of starts made while rostered by drafting team
Formula: (starts for the drafting team) / (weeks rostered by the drafting team) over the post-draft tenure window. Blank when the player was never rostered for an NFL week by the drafting team (no denominator); 0 when rostered but never started.
Notes: Roster + start weeks from player_week. Pick analogue of the transactions '% of starts made while rostered'.

Stat: Injury adjusted % of starts made while rostered by drafting team
Formula: (starts in injury/bye-free weeks) / (injury/bye-free weeks rostered) over the same window — divides only by weeks the player was available. Blank when there were no injury-free rostered weeks (or an unmade pick).
Notes: Removes weeks the player couldn't have started (injury/bye) from the denominator, like the transactions injury-adjusted variant.

Formula: Σ of the drafted player's fantasy points over the weeks they STARTED for the drafting team (Team) within the tenure window (draft anchor → next exit). Blank for an unmade pick; a number ≥ 0 for every made pick (0 if the player never started here / can't be resolved).
Notes: Pick analogue of the trades 'Points added' (received-starter output), restricted to the player drafted with this pick.

Formula: Points added / number of weeks the drafted player started for this team in the window. Blank for an unmade pick; 0 for a made pick with no started weeks.
Notes: Per-start average of the started-week output.

Stat: Avg points added adjusted by position
Formula: (Σ position-adjusted started-week points) / started weeks, where each week's points are × league_starter_avg / pos_avg[position]. Blank for an unmade pick; 0 for a made pick with no started weeks.
Notes: Position-normalised variant of Avg points added.

Formula: True if this pick's ownership shift wasn't recorded as a normal trade transaction. Detected when traded_picks_by_season shows a pick belonging to a non-original owner but no trade event in pick_trade_events explains the move (typical for picks moved >3 years before draft year, beyond Sleeper's trade-tracking window).
Notes: Such picks are NOT added to trades.csv (the move wasn't a trade); the assumption is a single move from original owner to current owner.

Formula: Count of times the pick changed hands by TRADE: recorded Sleeper trades PLUS off-platform commissioner moves (real trades Sleeper dropped because they happened beyond its ~3-year tracking window — the commissioner re-applied them by hand). The full ownership lineage — original owner, every trade WITH its full deal, each commissioner move, the draft, and then the drafted player's entire post-draft career — lives in the hover-comment on the pick's first cell (identical to the comment on that player's player_all_time row).
Notes: Replaces the former per-hop 'Trade 1 / Trade 2 / …' chain columns. The 2020 ESPN startup draft, the 2021 supplemental veteran draft, and the synthetic award picks (2.09 toilet reward, 5.xx FAAB buy) are all AWARDS/non-tradeable, not trades, so they count 0 here even though the 2.09's commissioner-assignment still shows in the comment.

Formula: On picks, the drafted PLAYER's next event after the draft — their first transaction '#N' or trade 'T#N' — a clickable row pointer in the xlsx. The draft row is the player chain's START, so 'next' walks forward into the player's career.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a player with no post-draft events. Uses the same '#N' / 'T#N' / 'PH#N' ref scheme as the transactions/trades links.

Formula: On picks, the PICK's last trade before the draft ('T#N') — a clickable row pointer in the xlsx. The draft row is the pick chain's TERMINAL, so 'previous' walks back into the pick's trade history.
Notes: Blank for an unmade or never-traded pick. Uses the same '#N' / 'T#N' / 'PH#N' ref scheme as the transactions/trades links.

Sheet: transactions / trades / all transaction & trade counts
Formula: A transaction is dropped entirely when every player it moves nets to zero on its own roster within the same calendar day AND a commissioner action was part of that player-day. I.e. a single-day commissioner correction that leaves the roster exactly as it started.
Notes: Covers: commissioner add+drop of a player; a player a team dropped that the commissioner re-added same day; an add the commissioner immediately undid; and a trade the commissioner reversed. These no-ops are excluded from every transaction/trade count and from the transactions.csv / trades.csv detail.

Formula: The player's real NFL team that week, resolved deterministically: nflverse week-specific stats team → that season's nflverse stats team → nflverse WEEKLY ROSTER team (catches players on a roster but with no stats — IR / suspended / PUP, e.g. Calvin Ridley 2022 on JAX while suspended) → the '33rd' sentinel "NFL" only when the player has an NFL identity (gsis_id) but was on NO roster that season (a true free agent or retired). Players with no gsis (team DSTs / unmapped) keep Sleeper's team field.
Notes: The "NFL" sentinel replaces the old live-Sleeper-snapshot fallback, which returned the player's CURRENT team (wrong for a past season) and churned between builds for free agents — e.g. Odell Beckham 2022 flipped MIA↔NYG, cascading through his bye → Hardship → z-scored Luck across every team. A sentinel-team player is never flagged on bye (it has no schedule).

Formula: True if this player STARTED this week AND is a handcuff that week: their own last-5 avg is < 10, and a same-NFL-team/same-position teammate who averages ≥ 10 PPG more (over last 5 played games) is injured/suspended. The injured teammate does NOT need to have been a starter.
Notes: Item 10: an 'activated' cuff is one that BECOMES A STARTER. The broader 'rostered handcuff' condition (same logic without the start requirement) drives team_*.'Number of cuffs rostered'; this started version drives 'Number of cuffs started'. team_year/all_time + league_year/all_time count DISTINCT cuff players, not player-weeks.

Formula: (Starter's points) − (highest-scoring eligible bench player who could have been started instead) in the same week.
Notes: Measures whether the lineup decision was correct. Negative = bench out-scored the starter (bad call).

Formula: (Bench player's points) − (lowest-scoring starter that could have been benched).
Notes: Mirror metric for bench rows.

Formula: Per-week: (1/6)*(1 - (AvgPF - 2/3*L_PF) / (L_PF/3)) + (1/6)*(1 - (AvgMaxPF - L_PF) / (L_MaxPF - L_PF)) + (1/6)*(1 - (AvgAge - 21) / (L_AvgAge - 21)) + (1/6)*pick_sum_this_year + (1/9)*future_draft_capital. AvgPF/MaxPF/Age are season-to-date expanding means through that week; L_* are league-wide season-to-date averages. team_year.Tanking = final week's expanding-mean value. team_all_time.Tanking = mean of per-season values.
Notes: Positive when the team is under-scoring vs league while accumulating draft capital. Per-week values aggregate by 'last week' (team_year) and 'mean of seasons' (team_all_time) — never summed, since each weekly value is already a season-to-date mean and summing would over-count.

Formula: Weekly = (0.27*OUT + 0.14*Sisenzweig - 0.14*Brosenzweig) * postboost + (0.36*OPP + 0.10*OWN) * GATE - 0.36*ADV + 0.12*EFF + 0.16*CLOSE - 0.25*LFH. LFH = 1 when the week is a 'Loss from hardship?' (a winnable game lost to injured starters), else 0 — a flat extra unlucky hit (~one typical weekly luck swing) on top of the ADV adversity term. OUT = Win(1/0.5/0) - pregame_p, where pregame_p = logistic(1.5 * mean of standardized full-season [MaxPF, PF, win%] differences vs opponent) — calibrated so winning to your talent nets ~0. OPP = z_week(-(opp PF - opponent's full-season avg PF)); OWN = z_week(PF - own full-season avg PF); ADV = z_week(Hardship + Starter-adjusted Hardship + 3*players on bye); EFF = z_week(Efficiency); CLOSE = sign(Margin)*max(0, 1-|Margin|/8); GATE = 1/(1+|Margin|/15); postboost = 1.8 in championship-bracket weeks (Final/Semifinal/3rd Place) else 1. z_week standardizes within each (year, week), clipped to +-2.5 then /2.5. team_year.Luck = the plain SUM of that season's weekly luck (no win% multiplier). team_all_time 'Avg yearly luck' = the MEAN of the per-season Luck totals (NOT the sum over all weeks).
Notes: Captures the luck in a result: OUT rewards winning relative to a pregame talent estimate (upsets = lucky); the closeness-GATEd OPP/OWN credit an opponent collapsing / your studs popping only when it swung a close game (a blowout isn't luck); ADV is heavily subtracted so heavy injuries/byes = very bad luck; Bros/Sis accent bad-beats/steals; CLOSE rewards nail-biter wins. Because pregame_p nets out winning, summing gives a season stat that tracks Win-Variance-style over/under-achievement (a juggernaut champion is only mildly lucky; a low-scoring overachiever is very lucky). All-time uses the AVERAGE of seasonal luck rather than a raw sum: weekly luck is ~zero-sum, but adversity is a persistent team trait, so summing every week ever lets a chronically healthy/injured team pile up unbounded luck that also grows with tenure — averaging keeps all-time on a single-season scale and fair across differing tenures. Full derivation + scorecard in plan/LUCK_REWORK.md.

Formula: Fantasy points the team LOST to injury/suspension: per (team, week), the sum over EVERY rostered player who missed the week to injury or suspension (scored 0, not on bye) — starters and bench alike — of their FULL expected-if-healthy points (their recent active-week scoring baseline, clamped ≥ 0). NOT gated on whether the player would have started, and not weighted by start share. team_year / team_all_time / league_week aggregate by SUMMING the weekly values.
Notes: Higher = more production knocked out by injuries/suspensions (NOT schedule strength). Byes are never counted. The starter-weighted variant is 'Starter-adjusted Hardship', which weights each missed player by the recent-started-share heuristic; plain Hardship applies no such gate (so Hardship ≥ Starter-adjusted Hardship). Both feed the heavy adversity term in Luck.

Formula: -1 × (standings_place − (pf_place + maxpf_place) / 2).
Notes: Negative when a team finishes better than their PF / Max PF percentile would predict (over-performance via luck or close-game wins).

Formula: Sample-size-shrunk mean O-Score of the picks the team MADE (picks.Team — the roster that made the selection, formerly 'Final Team'): skill = (n·mean + 5·50)/(n+5), pulled toward the league-neutral 50. Per (Team, Year) and all-time.
Notes: Higher = better drafting. The shrinkage keeps a 2-great-pick manager from out-ranking a 25-solid one. Blank with no made picks; vet / blank-O-Score picks drop out of the mean.

Formula: Sample-size-shrunk mean O-Score of the trades the team was in (trades.Team): skill = (n·mean + 5·50)/(n+5).
Notes: Higher = better trading. Blank with no trades; one-sided / untracked trades with a blank O-Score drop out.

Formula: Sample-size-shrunk WEIGHTED-mean O-Score of the team's waiver/FA transactions (transactions.Team): skill = (Σw·O-Score + 5·50)/(Σw + 5). Adds and swaps carry weight 1; pure drops (drop-only rows) carry weight 1/3, so they still register but a flurry of drops can't dominate.
Notes: Higher = better pickups. Blank with no transactions. Pure-drop moves ARE included, weighted 1/3 vs adds/swaps; only blank-O-Score moves drop out entirely.

Formula: Schedule-luck-free win rate: each week, score the team against EVERY other team (not just its actual opponent). Win % = (Σ over weeks of teams with strictly lower PF that week) / (Σ over weeks of other teams). Per (Team, Year) on team_year; pooled over all weeks on team_all_time. Ties count as neither a win nor a loss but stay in the denominator (so they depress the rate). Format 0–1, 4 decimals like Win %.
Notes: Reveals true scoring strength independent of who you were scheduled against — a team can be top-half (the existing flag) yet have a mediocre all-play %, or vice versa. Blank for a (team, year) with no games (e.g. the not-yet-played current/future season).

Formula: All-play win % − actual Win % (team_all_time uses 'All time win %'). Positive = the team's actual record UNDER-shot its all-play scoring strength (unlucky schedule / lost close games); negative = the record OVER-shot it (lucky schedule / won close games).
Notes: A compact schedule-luck read. Blank when all-play % is blank (no games).

Formula: Clutch index (PF): the team's average points-for per game in the WINNERS'-bracket playoffs (Semifinal / Final / 3rd Place) minus its average in the regular season, all-time. Positive = raises their scoring in the playoffs.
Notes: team_all_time only (#9). Blank for a team that never reached the winners' bracket. The toilet/consolation bracket is excluded.

Formula: Clutch index (win %): the team's win rate in the winners'-bracket playoffs minus its regular-season win rate, all-time. Positive = wins more in the playoffs.
Notes: team_all_time only (#9). Blank for a team that never made the winners' bracket.

Formula: TRUE when a LOSS would have flipped to a win had the team's hurt would-be-starters been available. Build a lineup from the ACTUAL STARTERS (real points) PLUS the injured/suspended players who missed (subbed in at their starter-adjusted hardship value), take the best valid lineup (bounded to lineup slots), and flag if that score beats the opponent's actual PF.
Notes: Healthy BENCH players are excluded — this asks 'what if their hurt guys were available', not 'what if they'd start/sat optimally'. Injury + suspension, never byes. Each flagged week also subtracts 0.25 from that week's Luck.

Formula: Count of 'Loss from hardship?' weeks per (Team, Year) / per Team all-time.
Notes: Blank for a (team, year) with no games; a real 0 stays 0.

Formula: Standard deviation of the player's points over his STARTED weeks only (bench weeks excluded).
Notes: Blank for a player who never started, and with fewer than 2 started weeks. player_year per season; player_all_time pools every started week.

Formula: The lowest single started-week points the player scored (absolute minimum over started weeks, not a percentile).
Notes: Blank for a player who never started.

Formula: The highest single started-week points the player scored (absolute maximum over started weeks).
Notes: Blank for a player who never started.

Formula: Share of the player's ACTIVE started weeks whose score landed in the BOOM tier — the top 10% (>= p90) of the ALL-TIME pooled distribution of active starter scores for that player's position (each week judged against its own position's bars). Position-relative, so it's comparable across positions.
Notes: The 5 Starter tier shares (boom / upper quartile / middle 50% / lower quartile / bust) sum to ~100%. Keeps a real 0 for a player who started but never boomed; blank if he never had an active start.

Formula: Share of the player's active started weeks in the UPPER-QUARTILE tier — between the position's p75 and p90 all-time starter scores (above the middle 50%, below a boom).
Notes: Part of the 5-tier positional split; blank with no active starts.

Formula: Share of the player's active started weeks in the MIDDLE-50% tier — between the position's p25 and p75 all-time starter scores (the interquartile range).
Notes: Part of the 5-tier positional split; blank with no active starts.

Formula: Share of the player's active started weeks in the LOWER-QUARTILE tier — between the position's p10 and p25 all-time starter scores (below the middle 50%, above a bust).
Notes: Part of the 5-tier positional split; blank with no active starts.

Formula: Share of the player's ACTIVE started weeks whose score landed in the BUST tier — the bottom 10% (<= p10) of the position's all-time pooled active-starter scores.
Notes: Position-relative (a TE bust bar sits lower than a QB's). Keeps a real 0 for a player who started but never busted; blank if he never had an active start.

Formula: Position-adjusted league-wide percentile (0–100) of Starter scoring volatility INVERTED — the steadiest (lowest volatility) player at his position = 100. player_year ranks within (season, position); player_all_time within position across all players.
Notes: #10. Blank with no underlying volatility (never started / <2 starts). Ties share the average rank.

Formula: Position-adjusted league-wide percentile of Starter scoring floor — the highest floor at the position = 100.
Notes: #10. Blank for a player who never started. Ties share the average rank.

Formula: Position-adjusted league-wide percentile of Starter scoring ceiling — the highest ceiling at the position = 100.
Notes: #10. Blank for a player who never started. Ties share the average rank.

Formula: Points Above Replacement summed over started weeks. Each week the replacement level = the mean of the BOTTOM THIRD of that week's STARTED scores at the player's position; PAR_week = his points − that level; Starter PAR = the Σ.
Notes: Value over a freely-startable option, week by week. Blank for a player who never started.

Formula: The mean (not sum) of the per-started-week PAR — average points above a last-startable replacement.
Notes: Blank for a player who never started.

Formula: Standard deviation of the player's points over every week he actually PLAYED while rostered — started OR benched — excluding bye/injury/suspension weeks.
Notes: Companion to the started-only set, including the low weeks a manager benched him for. Blank with no played weeks (and <2 weeks).

Formula: The lowest single played-while-rostered week's points (started or benched; non-played weeks excluded).
Notes: Blank with no played weeks.

Formula: The highest single played-while-rostered week's points (started or benched).
Notes: Blank with no played weeks.

Formula: Share of played-while-rostered weeks (started OR benched) whose score landed in the BOOM tier — top 10% (>= p90) of the position's all-time pooled active-starter scores (same positional bars as 'Starter boom %').
Notes: The 5 Rostered tier shares sum to ~100%. Real 0 if he played but never boomed; blank with no played weeks.

Formula: Share of played-while-rostered weeks in the UPPER-QUARTILE tier (position p75-p90), same bars as the Starter version but over every played week, started or benched.
Notes: Part of the 5-tier positional split; blank with no played weeks.

Formula: Share of played-while-rostered weeks in the MIDDLE-50% tier (position p25-p75 interquartile range).
Notes: Part of the 5-tier positional split; blank with no played weeks.

Formula: Share of played-while-rostered weeks in the LOWER-QUARTILE tier (position p10-p25).
Notes: Part of the 5-tier positional split; blank with no played weeks.

Formula: Share of played-while-rostered weeks (started OR benched) whose score landed in the BUST tier — bottom 10% (<= p10) of the position's all-time pooled active-starter scores.
Notes: Position-relative. Real 0 if he played but never busted; blank with no played weeks.

Formula: Where the week's score places (0-100) within the ALL-TIME pooled distribution of ACTIVE starter scores for the player's position — percent of that position's starter weeks scoring at or below this week. Drives the 5 scoring tiers (Bust <= p10, Lower quartile p10-p25, Middle 50% p25-p75, Upper quartile p75-p90, Boom >= p90).
Notes: Blank on bye / injury / suspension weeks (a 0 there isn't a real score), and for a position with no reference starter scores.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive STARTED weeks whose Positional scoring percentile put the player in that tier (Boom / Upper quartile / Middle 50% / Lower quartile / Bust). Terminal-encoded like the other player streaks: the length shows on the run's final week, 'In Progress' before, 0 on a started week in a different tier.
Notes: Bye / injury / suspension weeks — and benched weeks — are skipped (the run bridges across them; those cells read blank). Spans seasons.

Formula: Like the Starter tier streaks but over every PLAYED week (started OR benched): consecutive played weeks in that positional scoring tier, terminal-encoded.
Notes: Bye / injury / suspension weeks are skipped (blank; the run bridges them). Spans seasons.

Stat: % of starters in each positional scoring tier
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Share of ACTIVE starters whose score fell in each of the 5 positional scoring tiers — on the team sheets over the TEAM's starters, on the league sheets over EVERY team's starters league-wide — that week (…_week), pooled over the season (…_year), or pooled all-time (…_all_time). Each starter is judged against its own position's all-time starter-score bars; the 5 shares sum to ~100%.
Notes: Bye / injury / suspension starts are excluded (no real score). Blank for a week/period with no active starters.

Formula: Position-adjusted league-wide percentile of Rostered scoring volatility INVERTED (steadiest = 100), ranked like the Starter percentiles.
Notes: Blank with no underlying volatility. Ties share the average rank.

Formula: Position-adjusted league-wide percentile of Rostered scoring floor (highest = 100).
Notes: Blank with no played weeks. Ties share the average rank.

Formula: Position-adjusted league-wide percentile of Rostered scoring ceiling (highest = 100).
Notes: Blank with no played weeks. Ties share the average rank.

Formula: Weekly flag: TRUE for the team whose single top starter supplied the greatest share of its PF that week (highest '% of points (if starter)' among teams' top starters). One winner per league-week, ties shared.
Notes: Its top starter is that week's Captain.

Formula: Weekly flag: TRUE for the team with the highest total bench points that week. One winner per league-week, ties shared.
Notes: Mirrors the weekly-award pattern.

Formula: Weekly flag: TRUE for the team with the most injured players (starters + bench) that week. Needs ≥1 injury — a 0-injury week has no winner.
Notes: Ties shared.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team was the One-man army.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly flag.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team left the most points on its bench.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly flag.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team was the most injured.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly flag.

Formula: Weekly flag: TRUE for the single starter league-wide who supplied the highest share of his team's PF that week (max '% of points (if starter)'; alphabetical tie-break). The Captain's team is that week's One-man army.
Notes: One Captain per league-week.

Formula: Season (player_year) / career (player_all_time) count of weeks the player was the weekly Captain.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly Captain flag.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks this team had the week's highest team PF — does NOT reset between seasons. TERMINAL-ENCODED: the run's length shows only on its final/most-recent week; earlier weeks read 'In Progress', non-run weeks read 0 (so a descending sort surfaces each streak once).
Notes: Sortable top-N encoding.

Formula: All-time running count of consecutive weeks this team had the week's lowest team PF — does NOT reset between seasons. TERMINAL-ENCODED: the run's length shows only on its final/most-recent week; earlier weeks read 'In Progress', non-run weeks read 0 (so a descending sort surfaces each streak once).

Formula: Current consecutive wins WITHIN the season, as a running count each week.
Notes: Not terminal-encoded — a plain running streak that resets between seasons.

Formula: Current consecutive losses within the season, as a running count.
Notes: Not terminal-encoded; resets between seasons.

Formula: Consecutive wins carried ACROSS season boundaries (does not reset at year end).
Notes: Running count, not terminal-encoded.

Formula: Consecutive losses carried across season boundaries.
Notes: Running count, not terminal-encoded.

Formula: Consecutive seasons the team made the playoffs (top 4 by regular-season standings). ALL-TIME (doesn't reset) and terminal-encoded: the length shows on the final season of the run, 'In Progress' before, 0 when broken.
Notes: Season-grain (the season is its 'week'). A season with no games yet reads blank.

Formula: Consecutive seasons finishing ≥ .500 (Win % ≥ 0.5). ALL-TIME and terminal-encoded like the weekly streaks.
Notes: A current/future season with no games yet reads blank.

Formula: Across all opponents this team has actually played (≥1 all-time game), the maximum of its 'Win % vs <opponent>'.
Notes: Opponents never played are excluded. 'Team for highest Win %' holds the matching opponent.

Formula: Across all opponents actually played, the minimum 'Win % vs <opponent>'.
Notes: Opponents never played are excluded so 'lowest' isn't trivially 0.

Formula: The opponent handle against whom this team's all-time Win % is highest.
Notes: Links to that team's team_all_time row in the xlsx.

Formula: The opponent handle against whom this team's all-time Win % is lowest.
Notes: Among opponents actually played.

Formula: team_week: 1 if the team won while its pregame avg Max PF was below the opponent's (an upset win). League sheets sum those flags (week = upsets that week; year/all-time = total upsets).
Notes: Formerly duplicated on the league sheets as 'Number of wins with pregame avg max PF from opponent' — that redundant column was removed; UPST is the single source.

Formula: League-wide sum of team_week 'Number of starter donuts' (started players who actually played and scored exactly 0).
Notes: Companion to 'Number of donuts' (all rostered players). Starter-only version. Excludes bye / injury / suspension weeks — a 0 there is an absence, not a donut.

Formula: Max single-starter fantasy score league-wide over the period (week / season / all-time).
Notes: 'Difference between highest and lowest starters' is the gap to the lowest.

Formula: Min single-starter fantasy score league-wide over the period.
Notes: Can be negative (a started QB with a net-negative game).

Formula: DISTINCT trade events (by timestamp) the team / league was in that were dated BEFORE that season's kickoff (Sept 7). Each trade counts once regardless of how many teams were involved.
Notes: Year sheets per season; all-time sums across seasons (a trade lives in one season). Replaces the per-team 'Number of trades' which multi-counted multi-team trades.

Formula: DISTINCT trade events on/after that season's kickoff. Counted once per trade.
Notes: Year per season; all-time sums.

Formula: Offseason trades + Inseason trades — all distinct trade events the team / league was in.
Notes: Each multi-team trade counts once.

Formula: The fantasy team (manager) that rostered the player for the most TIME over the period (by rostered duration, not week count).
Notes: Manager name; links to its team_all_time row.

Formula: Alias of 'Top team' (capitalised header on player_year): the manager who rostered the player longest over the season.
Notes: Same definition as 'Top team'.

Formula: The fantasy team that held the player at the END of the season (in-season window only, so an offseason move doesn't overwrite it).
Notes: Manager name; links to its team_all_time row.

Formula: The player's age (decimal years) as of that week (birth_date vs the week's date). Per-season = mean weekly age (≈ mid-season).
Notes: Anchored to the week, not 'today', so historical weeks stay correct. Blank with no birth_date.

Formula: TRUE in the player's rookie NFL season.
Notes: Anchored to the season.

Formula: TRUE when the player was on a roster, scored 0, did not play, and it wasn't a bye or suspension. Primary source is the in-house weekly Sleeper injury tracker; nflverse + curated overlays back it up.
Notes: A player who scored > 0 is never flagged (you can play hurt). Suspension wins over injury; bye wins over both.

Formula: TRUE when the player was serving an NFL suspension that week.
Notes: Takes precedence over Injury?. byes excluded from Hardship.

Formula: TRUE when the player's NFL team had no game that week (he scored 0 with no game). Also TRUE for a rostered player with NO active NFL team (retired / unsigned / out of the league, NFL team = 'NFL') — no game exists for them either.
Notes: Wins over both Injury? and Suspension?; byes are excluded from Hardship and from played-week denominators. The no-NFL-team clause keeps retired meme-pickups (e.g. Brady '24/'25) out of the injury counts.

Formula: A started player's share of his team's starting PF that week = player points / team PF. Blank for bench players.
Notes: Drives the weekly Captain / One-man army awards.

Formula: The week's points minus the player's PRIOR PLAYED week's points (full-season NFLverse scoring).
Notes: Only emitted on a PLAYED week — a bye/injury/suspension week is blank (its 0 isn't a real score, so '0 − prior' would be a phantom drop); the next played week bridges across the gap. Blank in the first played week (no prior game).

Formula: The week's points minus the mean of the player's previous 5 PLAYED weeks.
Notes: Bye/injury/suspension non-played weeks are excluded from the baseline AND are themselves blank; blank before any prior games.

Stat: Change from career average to that point
Formula: The week's points minus the player's career PPG THROUGH the prior week (played weeks only).
Notes: Only emitted on a played week; bye/injury/suspension weeks are blank. Blank in the very first career week.

Formula: The week's points minus the player's FULL-career PPG (all played weeks).
Notes: Full-season scoring; non-played weeks are excluded from the baseline AND are themselves blank.

Formula: Running count of consecutive weeks the player has been rostered by his current team, through that week.
Notes: Resets when he joins a new team.

Formula: One player's whole tenure with one team, terminal-encoded: the value shows on his last week for that team and 'In Progress' before, so a sort ranks each player-team tenure once (a player can rank once per team). '% of starts' = started ÷ rostered weeks; '% of team points' = his starter points ÷ the team's starter PF (0-1); the rest are weeks / points / PPG as a starter or on the bench for that team, and weeks as the team's top / bottom starter.
Notes: Separate stints with the same team share one tenure. Each column has a '… this season' sibling that resets per year.

Formula: Season / career totals across all teams. Rostered = started + benched weeks; '% of starts' = started ÷ rostered; 'Total points as starter' = his starter points; '% of league points' = those points ÷ the whole league's starter scoring for the period (0-1).
Notes: 'Weeks as starter', 'PPG starter' and 'Times as Highest/Lowest starter on team?' are the other per-player (non-team) versions.

Stat: Number of consecutive weeks on bench before start (if starter)
Formula: On a week the player STARTS, how many consecutive weeks he sat on the bench immediately beforehand.
Notes: Only populated on a starting week.

Stat: Number of consecutive weeks on bench before start excluding injury/bye (if starter)
Formula: Same as the previous column, but weeks the player was unavailable (injury / bye / suspension) don't count as 'benched'.
Notes: Only populated on a starting week.

Stat: Difference from best startable bench (if starter)
Formula: For a STARTER: his points minus the best-scoring eligible bench player he could have started instead (the bench player's name is in 'Reference player name').
Notes: A negative value means a benched player out-scored him.

Stat: Difference from worst benchable starter (if bench)
Formula: For a BENCH player: his points minus the weakest starter he could have replaced.
Notes: A positive value means he out-scored a starter he sat behind.

Formula: The specific player the start/sit difference is measured against that week (the best startable bench player, or the worst benchable starter).
Notes: Hyperlinks to that player's player_week row for the same (year, week) — the Phase-11 per-week link exception.

Stat: Difference in averages of best/worst startables over previous 5 games
Formula: The same best-bench / worst-starter comparison, but on each side's trailing-5-game average instead of that week's single score.
Notes: Smooths out one-week noise in the start/sit signal.

Formula: The start/sit difference folded together with the handcuff signal (whether a much-higher-upside cuff was available).
Notes: Pairs with '- Activated Cuff?'.

Stat: - Activated Cuff? (Was a player of the same nfl team/position & who averages >10 PPG more over last 5 played games injured? Only for players with avg <10 PPG)
Formula: Flags that a rostered handcuff — same NFL team + position, averaging >10 PPG more over the last 5 played games — was started while its starter was hurt (only considered for players averaging <10 PPG).
Notes: Feeds the Cuff adjusted difference.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE the week the player was the highest-scoring starter league-wide. One winner per (Year, Week) per award, alphabetical tie-break.
Notes: Season/career count is 'Times as Player of the week?'; the companion streak is in the Streaks family.

Stat: Times as Player of the week?
Formula: Season (player_year) / career (player_all_time) count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring starter league-wide.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Player of the week?' flag.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE the week the player was the highest-scoring QB starter. One winner per (Year, Week) per award, alphabetical tie-break.
Notes: Season/career count is 'Times as QB of the week?'; the companion streak is in the Streaks family.

Stat: Times as QB of the week?
Formula: Season (player_year) / career (player_all_time) count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring QB starter.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'QB of the week?' flag.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE the week the player was the highest-scoring RB starter. One winner per (Year, Week) per award, alphabetical tie-break.
Notes: Season/career count is 'Times as RB of the week?'; the companion streak is in the Streaks family.

Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'RB of the week?' flag.

Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'TE of the week?' flag.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE the week the player was the lowest-scoring starter (no winner if 2+ tie at 0). One winner per (Year, Week) per award, alphabetical tie-break.
Notes: Season/career count is 'Times as Benchwarmer of the week?'; the companion streak is in the Streaks family.

Stat: Times as Benchwarmer of the week?
Formula: Season (player_year) / career (player_all_time) count of weeks the player was the lowest-scoring starter (no winner if 2+ tie at 0).
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Benchwarmer of the week?' flag.

Formula: Season (player_year) / career (player_all_time) count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring QB on a bench.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Bench QB of the week?' flag.
