Formula: Season (player_year) / career (player_all_time) count of weeks the player was the highest-scoring TE on a bench.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Bench TE of the week?' flag.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE the week the player was its team's highest-scoring starter that week. One winner per (Year, Week) per award, alphabetical tie-break.
Notes: Season/career count is 'Times as Highest starter on team?'; the companion streak is in the Streaks family.

Stat: Times as Highest starter on team?
Formula: Season (player_year) / career (player_all_time) count of weeks the player was its team's highest-scoring starter that week.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Highest starter on team?' flag.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE the week the player was its team's lowest-scoring starter that week. One winner per (Year, Week) per award, alphabetical tie-break.
Notes: Season/career count is 'Times as Lowest starter on team?'; the companion streak is in the Streaks family.

Stat: Times as Lowest starter on team?
Formula: Season (player_year) / career (player_all_time) count of weeks the player was its team's lowest-scoring starter that week.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Lowest starter on team?' flag.

Sheet: player_week / player_year / player_all_time / team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of roster moves (adds + drops) involving the row's subject in the period. On player sheets it counts moves of that player; on team/league sheets the team's / league's moves, rolling week → year → all-time.
Notes: Identity rollup across grains.

Formula: Count of DROPS only involving the row's subject in the period.
Notes: Rolls week → year → all-time.

Sheet: player_week / player_year / player_all_time / team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of trades involving the row's subject in the period.
Notes: On the year/all-time TEAM/LEAGUE sheets this is superseded by the distinct Offseason/Inseason/Total trades (multi-team trades counted once).

Formula: Mean points over the weeks the subject ROSTERED the player (started or benched).
Notes: Derived consumers prefer the Adjusted variant.

Formula: Mean points per PLAYED week — bye/injury/suspension non-played weeks are dropped from the denominator.
Notes: The per-played-week average; what most derived metrics use.

Formula: The player's TOTAL NFLverse points that SEASON (every NFL game, including weeks he wasn't rostered here).
Notes: From NFLverse (re-scored with league settings), not LOTG rosters. On player_all_time the career analog is 'Points (full career)'.

Formula: The player's TOTAL NFLverse points across his whole CAREER (every NFL game in every season).
Notes: All-time analog of player_year's 'Points (full season)'; from NFLverse, not LOTG rosters.

Formula: Per-game mean of Points (full season) that season.
Notes: From NFLverse, not LOTG rosters.

Formula: Per-game mean of the player's career NFLverse points (Points (full career) / career games).
Notes: All-time analog of player_year's 'Avg points (full season)'; from NFLverse, not LOTG rosters.

Formula: Mean points in the player's STARTED weeks.
Notes: Blank if he never started that period.

Formula: Mean points in the player's BENCHED weeks.
Notes: Blank if he never benched that period.

Formula: PPG starter with bye/injury/suspension weeks dropped from the denominator (per-played-started-week).
Notes: Blank if he never started.

Formula: PPG bench with bye/injury/suspension weeks dropped from the denominator.
Notes: Blank if he never benched.

Formula: PPG starter − PPG bench — how much better the player scored when started vs benched.
Notes: Blank when he never started or never benched.

Formula: Distinct fantasy teams (managers) that rostered the player during the season / career, counting partial-week rosterings.
Notes: Phase 3 fix: counts every team that held the player at any point (Renfrow = 5).

Formula: Count of weeks the player was a starter over the period.
Notes: Per season / career.

Formula: Count of weeks the player was rostered, scored 0, and was flagged injured.
Notes: League sheets carry the league-wide 'Number of weeks missed due to injury'.

Formula: Count of weeks the player was rostered, scored 0, and was flagged suspended.
Notes: League sheets carry the league-wide 'Number of weeks missed due to suspensions'.

Stat: Change in points from previous season
Formula: This season's full-season total points minus the prior season's.
Notes: Blank for a rookie year / when there's no prior season.

Stat: Change in avg points from previous season
Formula: This season's full-season avg points minus the prior season's.
Notes: Blank with no prior season.

Formula: This season's full-season total points minus the player's career baseline.
Notes: Blank when there's no career baseline yet.

Stat: Change in avg points from career
Formula: All NFLverse: this season's full-NFL-season avg points (Points (full season) / season games) minus the player's prior-career avg (prior-career points / prior-career games). Both span every ACTIVE NFL week of the season/career — not just weeks rostered here — with bye/injury/suspension weeks naturally excluded (a week he didn't play has no NFLverse game record).
Notes: Blank with no career baseline. Also blank when the season has fewer than 2 NFL games: a single game is too small a sample to compare a per-game average against a whole-career average (one big game would read as a huge jump). The season TOTAL 'Change in points from career' is full-season and not gated this way.

Formula: TRUE if the player is in their FIRST LOTG season (their earliest player_year season equals the current/latest season) AND has never been a fantasy starter in any LOTG week — eligible to be stashed on a taxi/practice squad.
Notes: Keyed off BOTH conditions: first-year-in-league (so it resets at week 1 of the player's second season — a rookie finishing year 1 stays eligible until his first snap of year 2) AND Weeks-as-starter == 0. A never-started veteran from an earlier league season is therefore NOT taxi-eligible; only current-season newcomers who haven't started are.

Formula: The team's started-lineup points (points for) that week. LEAGUE RULE: in the Semifinal week (Week 15 in the 16-week 2020 season; Week 16 in the 17-week 2021+ seasons) each matchup's HIGHER SEED gets +5 homefield advantage added to PF, so Semifinal PF = Σ starters + 5 for the two higher seeds.
Notes: Rolls up to season/all-time totals and averages; used throughout standings and Luck.

Formula: The opponent's PF that week (the points scored against this team).
Notes: Drives Margin and Differential.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The team's best-possible (optimal) lineup score that week from its rostered players.
Notes: Used for Efficiency and the optimal-lineup metrics.

Formula: PF − Points against (positive = won by that much).
Notes: Σ over the period = Differential.

Formula: True for a win, False for a loss (a tie would count as half a win in Win %; none has occurred yet).
Notes: Aggregates into Win % and Record — which count EVERY game (regular season + playoff/toilet brackets).

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: PF / Max PF — how close the actual lineup came to the optimal one.
Notes: ≤ 1; drives the Most/Least efficient awards.

Formula: League in-joke flag: TRUE when the team LOST while being the 2nd-HIGHEST scoring team of the week (the most unlucky loss — only the week's top scorer outscored you).
Notes: Feeds the Luck model.

Formula: League in-joke flag: TRUE when the team WON while being the 2nd-LOWEST scoring team of the week (the luckiest win — only the week's bottom scorer scored less than you).
Notes: Feeds the Luck model.

Formula: Season / career count of the team's Brosenzweig weeks (losses while the 2nd-highest scorer of the week).
Notes: Rolls up the weekly flag.

Formula: Season / career count of the team's Sisenzweig weeks (wins while the 2nd-lowest scorer of the week).
Notes: Rolls up the weekly flag.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE for the team with the maximum PF that week. One winner per (Year, Week), ties shared.
Notes: Companion streak is in the Streaks family; the season/career count is 'Times Highest score?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team was the team with the maximum PF.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Highest score?' flag.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE for the team with the minimum PF that week. One winner per (Year, Week), ties shared.
Notes: Companion streak is in the Streaks family; the season/career count is 'Times Lowest score?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team was the team with the minimum PF.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Lowest score?' flag.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE for the team that won by the smallest margin that week. One winner per (Year, Week), ties shared.
Notes: Companion streak is in the Streaks family; the season/career count is 'Times Narrowest victory?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team was the team that won by the smallest margin.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Narrowest victory?' flag.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE for the team that won by the largest margin that week. One winner per (Year, Week), ties shared.
Notes: Companion streak is in the Streaks family; the season/career count is 'Times Largest blowout?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team was the team that won by the largest margin.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Largest blowout?' flag.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE for the team with the maximum Efficiency (PF / Max PF) that week. One winner per (Year, Week), ties shared.
Notes: Companion streak is in the Streaks family; the season/career count is 'Times Most efficient?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team was the team with the maximum Efficiency (PF / Max PF).
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Most efficient?' flag.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE for the team with the minimum Efficiency that week. One winner per (Year, Week), ties shared.
Notes: Companion streak is in the Streaks family; the season/career count is 'Times Least efficient?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team was the team with the minimum Efficiency.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Least efficient?' flag.

Formula: Weekly award flag: TRUE for a team with PF at or above the week's median that week. One winner per (Year, Week), ties shared.
Notes: Companion streak is in the Streaks family; the season/career count is 'Times Top half of league?'.

Formula: Season / career count of weeks the team was a team with PF at or above the week's median.
Notes: Rolls up the weekly 'Top half of league?' flag.

Stat: Increase in points from previous week
Formula: PF minus the prior week's PF (Week 1 compares to last season's final week).
Notes: Rolls up to season averages.

Formula: Number of roster slots that changed vs the prior week: max(this week's roster size, last week's) minus the players common to both.
Notes: One-directional slot count — it does NOT double-count each swap (unlike the season-level Inseason/Offseason turnover, which sum departures + arrivals). Ceiling ≈ the larger roster's size. Averages to 'Average weekly roster turnover' on the year/all-time sheets.

Formula: Number of starting-lineup slots that changed vs the prior week: max(this week's starters, last week's) minus the starters common to both.
Notes: One-directional slot count — it does NOT double-count each swap (unlike the season-level turnover metrics, which sum drops + adds). Averages to 'Average weekly starter turnover'.

Stat: Difference in pregame avg max PF from opponent
Formula: The team's trailing average Max PF minus the opponent's — a pregame talent gauge.
Notes: Positive = the stronger recent roster on paper.

Formula: Count of the team's injured players (starters + bench) that week.
Notes: League/year/all-time carry the rolled-up versions.

Formula: Count of the team's suspended players that week.
Notes: Byes are tracked separately and excluded from Hardship.

Formula: Count of the team's players whose NFL team was on bye that week.
Notes: Byes are excluded from Hardship.

Formula: Count of the team's would-be-STARTERS who were injured that week.
Notes: Feeds Starter-adjusted Hardship and Loss from hardship?.

Formula: Count of the team's would-be-STARTERS who were suspended that week.
Notes: Feeds Starter-adjusted Hardship.

Formula: Expected points lost from injured/suspended would-be-starters, each weighted by how likely it was to start (effective starter %).
Notes: Byes excluded. Drives the hardship-loss counterfactual.

Formula: Weekly composite of how much a team's RESULT diverged from its performance/schedule: rewards unlucky losses / strong-but-beaten weeks and penalizes lucky wins, gated by win/loss, with opponent-strength, own-strength, closeness, efficiency and a losses-from-hardship penalty. team_year Luck = Σ weekly Luck (see plan/LUCK_REWORK.md for the exact weighting).
Notes: Each loss-from-hardship week subtracts 0.25. Calibrated so winning-to-talent nets ≈ 0.

Formula: Wins + ½·ties over ALL games played that season — regular season AND playoff/toilet-bracket games (16 games in the completed 2020 season, 17 in each completed 2021+ season).
Notes: 0–1 fraction.

Formula: The team's 'W-L' record across ALL games that season — regular season AND playoff/toilet-bracket games (16 in the completed 2020 season, 17 in each completed 2021+ season).
Notes: Text companion to Win %.

Formula: The team's pooled 'W-L' record across every season (all games incl. playoff/toilet brackets).
Notes: All-time pool of the per-season Record.

Formula: The team's pooled win rate across every season (all games incl. playoff/toilet brackets).
Notes: 0–1 fraction.

Formula: The team's 'W-L' record in REGULAR-SEASON games only (the 'Week N' matchups; playoff and toilet-bowl bracket games excluded). Per season on team_year; pooled across all seasons on team_all_time.
Notes: The standings record. The plain 'Record' / 'All time record' include every game (regular + both brackets).

Formula: (wins + ½·ties) / regular-season games — win rate over the 'Week N' games only.
Notes: Blank for a (team, year) with no regular-season games yet (e.g. an in-progress 2026).

Formula: The team's all-time 'W-L' record in WINNERS'-bracket CHAMPIONSHIP-path games (Semifinal + Final). The 3rd-place consolation game is NOT counted here (see 'Third place game record').
Notes: '0-0' for a team that has never reached the winners' bracket. The toilet/consolation bracket is excluded.

Formula: (wins + ½·ties) / winners'-bracket Semifinal+Final games — all-time championship-path win rate (3rd-place game excluded).
Notes: Blank for a team that never reached the winners' bracket (no playoff games).

Formula: The team's all-time 'W-L' record in LOSERS'-bracket games that decide the toilet champion (Toilet Semis + Toilet Final). The toilet-losers game is NOT counted here (see 'Toilet losers game record').
Notes: '0-0' for a team that has never landed in the toilet bracket.

Formula: (wins + ½·ties) / Toilet Semis+Final games — all-time toilet-bowl win rate (toilet-losers game excluded).
Notes: Blank for a team that has never been in the toilet bracket.

Formula: The team's all-time 'W-L' record in the 3rd-PLACE consolation game (played by the two Semifinal losers). Small N by design.
Notes: '0-0' for a team that has never played a 3rd-place game. Kept separate from Playoff record.

Formula: The team's all-time 'W-L' record in the TOILET-LOSERS game (Toilet Trash — the two Toilet-Semis losers play to avoid last place). Small N by design.
Notes: '0-0' for a team that has never played the toilet-losers game. Kept separate from Toilet bowl record.

Formula: Σ Margin (PF − Points against) over the period.
Notes: Per season; all-time sums across seasons.

Formula: Per-game mean of Margin over the period.
Notes: Differential / games.

Formula: Head-to-head 'W-L' record in games vs that season's top-4 playoff seeds. team_all_time pools the per-season class memberships.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season (classes unknown until it ends).

Formula: Win rate in games vs that season's top-4 playoff seeds.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season; singular 'champion' is per-season, 'champions' is the all-time variant.

Formula: Head-to-head 'W-L' record in games vs that season's non-playoff teams. team_all_time pools the per-season class memberships.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season (classes unknown until it ends).

Formula: Win rate in games vs that season's non-playoff teams.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season; singular 'champion' is per-season, 'champions' is the all-time variant.

Formula: Head-to-head 'W-L' record in games vs that season's champion. team_all_time pools the per-season class memberships.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season (classes unknown until it ends).

Formula: Win rate in games vs that season's champion.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season; singular 'champion' is per-season, 'champions' is the all-time variant.

Formula: Head-to-head 'W-L' record in games vs the champions (all-time, pooling each season's champion). team_all_time pools the per-season class memberships.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season (classes unknown until it ends).

Formula: Win rate in games vs the champions (all-time, pooling each season's champion).
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season; singular 'champion' is per-season, 'champions' is the all-time variant.

Formula: Head-to-head 'W-L' record in games vs that season's last-place team. team_all_time pools the per-season class memberships.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season (classes unknown until it ends).

Formula: Win rate in games vs that season's last-place team.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season; singular 'champion' is per-season, 'champions' is the all-time variant.

Formula: The team's ordinal finish that season — Champion / 2nd / 3rd / 4th (decided in the winners' bracket) then 5th / 6th / 7th / 8th for the non-playoff teams, ranked by record (PF as tiebreaker) through the season's final game for 2020-2024 (Week 16 in the 16-week 2020 season; Week 17 in the 17-week 2021-2024 seasons) — which folds in the toilet bowl bracket, since by league design toilet-bowl results counted toward final standings those seasons — or through Week 15 for 2025+ (the true regular season only).
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season. The four bracket places come from the Final and 3rd-place games; 5th-8th are the standings order among teams that missed the top-4 bracket (8th = last place). Can disagree with the all-time last-place stats, which are always regular-season-only.

Formula: The regular-season week the team was mathematically eliminated from playoff (top-4 bracket) contention — the first week at which at least 4 other teams can no longer be caught even if this team wins out. 0 for the 4 teams that DID make the bracket (a bracket team has no elimination week); a team that missed the bracket carries the real week it was eliminated.
Notes: Blank for an in-progress season. Computed over the regular-season weeks only (weeks before the playoff start).

Formula: Career count of league titles won.
Notes: Not counted for an in-progress season.

Formula: Number of seasons the team made the top-4 playoff bracket.
Notes: In-progress seasons not counted.

Formula: Number of seasons the team reached the Final (champion or runner-up).
Notes: In-progress seasons not counted.

Formula: Number of seasons the team finished last.
Notes: In-progress seasons not counted.

Formula: Mean of the week-over-week roster turnover over the period.
Notes: All-time = per-season average, not a sum.

Formula: Mean of the week-over-week starting-lineup turnover.
Notes: All-time = per-season average.

Formula: Symmetric difference between the Week-1 roster and the championship-week roster — players who left PLUS players who arrived, counted separately. Spans the FULL roster (starters + bench).
Notes: Bidirectional, so each swap counts as 2 and the ceiling is roughly the two rosters' COMBINED size — not the roster size. With a deep bench a near-total rebuild can exceed 40. Per season; all-time averages.

Formula: Symmetric difference between the Week-1 starting lineup and the championship-week lineup — starters dropped PLUS starters added, counted separately.
Notes: Bidirectional; each swap counts as 2, so the ceiling is roughly the two lineups' combined size. Per season.

Formula: Symmetric difference between the prior season's championship-week roster and this season's Week-1 roster — players who left PLUS players who arrived, counted separately. Spans the FULL roster (starters + bench).
Notes: Bidirectional, so each swap counts as 2 and the ceiling is roughly the prior + new roster sizes — not the roster size. With a deep bench a near-total offseason rebuild can exceed 40 (e.g. plehv79 2025 = 43: 21 left + 22 arrived over ~31-man rosters). Per season; all-time averages.

Formula: Symmetric difference between the prior season's final starting lineup and this season's Week-1 starters — starters dropped PLUS starters added, counted separately.
Notes: Bidirectional; each swap counts as 2, so the ceiling is roughly the prior + new starter counts. Per season.

Formula: Σ pick-slot value of the picks the team actually MADE that season (a 1/(slot+1)-style curve).
Notes: Counts ROOKIE-draft selections only. Excludes BOTH non-rookie drafts: the 2020 ESPN startup draft (19 rounds) and the 2021 supplemental veteran draft.

Stat: Number of first round picks made
Formula: Count of FIRST-round picks the team made that season.
Notes: Rookie-draft picks only. Excludes BOTH non-rookie drafts: the 2020 ESPN startup draft and the 2021 supplemental veteran draft.

Formula: Count of ALL picks the team made that season.
Notes: Rookie-draft picks only. Excludes BOTH non-rookie drafts: the 2020 ESPN startup draft and the 2021 supplemental veteran draft.

Formula: Of the players on a team's WEEK-1 roster in year Y, the FRACTION still on that team's WEEK-1 roster in year Y+3 (|roster_Y ∩ roster_{Y+3}| / |roster_Y|). A rate, not a count, because roster sizes have grown over the years. team_year carries the rate for that season's class; team_all_time = the average of a team's measurable yearly rates.
Notes: Improvement #16. Blank when the Y+3 week-1 roster doesn't exist yet — measurable only for source years Y whose Y+3 season has been played (currently 2020→2023, 2021→2024 and 2022→2025; Y of 2023 or later is still pending its Y+3 season). Simple roster-snapshot retention: a player traded away and re-acquired by Y+3 week 1 still counts.

Formula: Value of the picks the team currently HOLDS for the next 3 years (own retained + acquired − traded away).
Notes: team_week updates on each trade; team_year uses the season-end snapshot.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Σ winning waiver bids over the period.
Notes: League sheets carry the league-wide total.

Stat: Change in win % from previous season
Formula: This season's Win % minus last season's.
Notes: Blank for a team's first season.

Formula: Mean of the team's season Luck totals (all-time).
Notes: League sheets carry the league-wide version.

Formula: Per-game mean of PF over the period.
Notes: League sheets = league-wide average.

Formula: Per-game mean of Max PF over the period.
Notes: League sheets = league-wide average.

Formula: Per-game mean of Points against over the period.
Notes: League sheets = league-wide average.

Formula: Per-game mean of Margin over the period.
Notes: League sheets = league-wide average.

Formula: Season/career sum of the team's injured player-weeks.
Notes: Starter variant uses the same starter heuristic as Starter-adjusted Hardship.

Formula: Season/career sum of the team's would-be-STARTER injured player-weeks.
Notes: Starter heuristic = effective-starter weighting.

Formula: Season/career sum of the team's would-be-STARTER suspended player-weeks.
Notes: Starter heuristic as above.

Formula: Season/career sum of the team's suspended player-weeks.
Notes: All rostered suspended players.

Stat: Number of weeks missed due to injury
Formula: League-wide total injured player-weeks over the period.
Notes: League companion to the team 'Weeks of injuries'.

Stat: Number of weeks missed due to suspensions
Formula: League-wide total suspended player-weeks over the period.
Notes: League companion to 'Weeks suspensions'.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of QBs the team started that week.
Notes: Week sheets are per-week counts; year/all-time count DISTINCT players over the period. League sheets carry the league-wide figure.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of QBs the team rostered that week.
Notes: Week sheets are per-week counts; year/all-time count DISTINCT players over the period. League sheets carry the league-wide figure.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of RBs the team started that week.
Notes: Week sheets are per-week counts; year/all-time count DISTINCT players over the period. League sheets carry the league-wide figure.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of RBs the team rostered that week.
Notes: Week sheets are per-week counts; year/all-time count DISTINCT players over the period. League sheets carry the league-wide figure.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of WRs the team started that week.
Notes: Week sheets are per-week counts; year/all-time count DISTINCT players over the period. League sheets carry the league-wide figure.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of WRs the team rostered that week.
Notes: Week sheets are per-week counts; year/all-time count DISTINCT players over the period. League sheets carry the league-wide figure.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of TEs the team started that week.
Notes: Week sheets are per-week counts; year/all-time count DISTINCT players over the period. League sheets carry the league-wide figure.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of TEs the team rostered that week.
Notes: Week sheets are per-week counts; year/all-time count DISTINCT players over the period. League sheets carry the league-wide figure.

Stat: Most number of players started from same NFL team
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's started players that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Stat: Most number of players rostered from same NFL team
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's rostered players that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Stat: Most number of QBs started from same NFL team
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's started QBs that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Stat: Most number of QBs rostered from same NFL team
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's rostered QBs that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Stat: Most number of RBs started from same NFL team
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's started RBs that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Stat: Most number of RBs rostered from same NFL team
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's rostered RBs that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Stat: Most number of WR started from same NFL team
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's started WR that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Stat: Most number of WR rostered from same NFL team
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's rostered WR that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Stat: Most number of TE started from same NFL team
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's started TE that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Stat: Most number of TE rostered from same NFL team
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The largest group of the team's rostered TE that share one NFL team that week.
Notes: Year/all-time take the max over the period; league sheets carry the league-wide max.

Stat: Number of NFL teams among starting players
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: How many distinct NFL teams the team's starting players represented that week.
Notes: Year/all-time = distinct NFL teams over the period; league sheets = league-wide distinct count.

Stat: Number of NFL teams among rostered players
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: How many distinct NFL teams the team's rostered players represented that week.
Notes: Year/all-time = distinct NFL teams over the period; league sheets = league-wide distinct count.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of rookies the team started that week.
Notes: Week = per-week; year/all-time = DISTINCT players; league sheets carry the league-wide figure.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of rookies the team rostered that week.
Notes: Week = per-week; year/all-time = DISTINCT players; league sheets carry the league-wide figure.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of handcuffs (cuffs) the team rostered that week.
Notes: Week = per-week; year/all-time = DISTINCT players; league sheets carry the league-wide figure.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of handcuffs (cuffs) the team started that week.
Notes: Week = per-week; year/all-time = DISTINCT players; league sheets carry the league-wide figure.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of the team's players who actually played and scored exactly 0 (a 'donut') that week.
Notes: Excludes bye / injury / suspension weeks — a 0 from an absent player is not a donut. Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Formula: Count of the team's starters who actually played and scored exactly 0 that week.
Notes: Excludes bye / injury / suspension weeks. Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of the team's players who scored under 10 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Formula: Count of the team's starters who scored under 10 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of the team's players who scored over 20 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Formula: Count of the team's starters who scored over 20 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of the team's players who scored over 30 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Formula: Count of the team's starters who scored over 30 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of the team's players who scored over 40 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Formula: Count of the team's starters who scored over 40 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Count of the team's players who scored over 50 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Formula: Count of the team's starters who scored over 50 that week.
Notes: Week = per-week count; year/all-time = sums over the period.

Stat: Difference between highest and lowest starters
Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: The team's top starter score minus its bottom starter score that week.
Notes: Week = per-week spread; year/all-time roll up.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: Mean age of the team's rostered players that week (year/all-time = mean of weekly averages, ≈ mid-season age).
Notes: Age anchored to the week date, not 'today'. League sheets carry the league-wide average.

Sheet: team_week / team_year / team_all_time / league_week / league_year / league_all_time
Formula: How many of the players the team drafted in the inaugural 2020 startup draft (the 19-round ESPN startup) it still rosters at the point in time.
Notes: Counts the surviving 2020-startup assets, keyed off each pick's drafted player_id. Startup picks weren't tradeable on ESPN, so the drafter is the pick's Original Team. (Distinct from the 2021 rookie/vet draft.)

Formula: Total league points scored that week (sum of every team's PF).
Notes: Year/all-time aggregate the weekly totals.

Formula: Max team PF − min team PF that week (the spread of team scores).
Notes: Year/all-time aggregate.

Formula: Max game margin − min game margin that week.
Notes: How lopsided the week's slate was.

Formula: Count of games decided by a final margin within 5 points.
Notes: Close-game tally; year/all-time sum.

Formula: Count of games decided by a final margin within 10 points.
Notes: Year/all-time sum.

Formula: The tightest regular-season PF margin that broke a SAME-RECORD seeding tie that season. Final regular-season standings rank by (wins + 0.5*ties) then total regular-season PF; among every pair of teams that finished adjacent in the standings with an identical record (so PF decided the seed), this is the smallest PF gap. Blank if no two teams shared a record (no tiebreaker was needed).
Notes: Surfaces how close the seeding race came to flipping on the PF tiebreaker. Smaller = tighter.

Formula: The deal's FIRST counterparty — the (or a) team this team traded with. In the xlsx it hyperlinks to that team's row of the SAME trade (matched on the shared Date).
Notes: A normal 2-team swap fills only this column; a multi-team trade also fills 'Team's traded with 2', etc.

Formula: The deal's SECOND counterparty (only on a 3+ team trade); links to that team's row of the same trade. Blank for a normal 2-team swap.
Notes: One column per additional counterparty, so each can be its own hyperlink (Excel can't link a substring of a lumped cell).

Formula: The player the pick was used on (blank/'Unknown' for an unmade pick).
Notes: Hyperlink target (Phase 11D).

Formula: The drafted player's KeepTradeCut superflex value (sf_trade_value) on draft day (≈ Aug 28 of the draft year).
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or when the date predates the player's KTC history.

Stat: KTC at end of rookie year
Formula: The drafted player's KTC superflex value at the end of their rookie season (≈ Feb 1 after the draft).
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a date before the player's KTC history begins.

Formula: The drafted player's KTC superflex value exactly one year after draft day.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a still-future checkpoint.

Formula: The drafted player's KTC superflex value exactly two years after draft day.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a still-future checkpoint.

Formula: The drafted player's KTC superflex value exactly three years after draft day.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a still-future checkpoint.

Formula: The drafted player's KTC superflex value exactly four years after draft day.
Notes: Blank for an unmade pick or a still-future checkpoint.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in Player addition value
Formula: The pick's realized Player addition value minus the average Player addition value of its slot's comparison set (the slot's expected return); positive = the pick beat its draft position.
Notes: Comparison baseline = the 3-pick window around the slot plus a 4th averaged-outer pick (from slot 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in Avg PPG on team adjusted by position
Formula: The pick's realized Avg PPG on team adjusted by position minus that stat's average across the slot's comparison set; positive = the pick out-produced its draft slot while on the drafting team.
Notes: Baseline = the 3-pick window around the slot + a 4th averaged-outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in Avg career PPG adjusted by position
Formula: The pick's realized Avg career PPG adjusted by position minus that stat's average across the slot's comparison set; positive = the player's whole-career production beat the draft slot.
Notes: Baseline = the 3-pick window around the slot + a 4th averaged-outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in Avg points added adjusted by position
Formula: The pick's realized Avg points added adjusted by position minus that stat's average across the slot's comparison set; positive = the pick's started-week output beat its draft slot.
Notes: Baseline = the 3-pick window around the slot + a 4th averaged-outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in KTC on draft day
Formula: The pick's KTC on draft day minus the average draft-day KTC of its slot's comparison set; positive = the player was valued above the slot's expectation at the draft.
Notes: Baseline = the 3-pick window around the slot + a 4th averaged-outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in KTC at end of rookie year
Formula: The pick's KTC at end of rookie year minus the average end-of-rookie-year KTC of its slot's comparison set; positive = beat the slot one rookie season in.
Notes: Baseline = the 3-pick window around the slot + a 4th averaged-outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in KTC 1 year after draft day
Formula: The pick's KTC 1 year after draft day minus that checkpoint's average across the slot's comparison set; positive = above slot expectation a year out.
Notes: Baseline = the 3-pick window around the slot + a 4th averaged-outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in KTC 2 years after draft day
Formula: The pick's KTC 2 years after draft day minus that checkpoint's average across the slot's comparison set; positive = above slot expectation two years out.
Notes: Baseline = the 3-pick window around the slot + a 4th averaged-outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in KTC 3 years after draft day
Formula: The pick's KTC 3 years after draft day minus that checkpoint's average across the slot's comparison set; positive = above slot expectation three years out.
Notes: Baseline = the 3-pick window around the slot + a 4th averaged-outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.

Stat: Pick-adjusted Difference in KTC 4 years after draft day
Formula: The pick's KTC 4 years after draft day minus that checkpoint's average across the slot's comparison set; positive = above slot expectation four years out.
Notes: Baseline = the 3-pick window around the slot + a 4th averaged-outer pick (from 1.05 on); 1.01–1.04 use the pooled mean.
